scope.description: Food-adjacent SECOP II contracts selected from the corruption/hunger pattern dataset, checked against public execution, invoice, payment, modification, guarantee, location, multi-year document rows, and SECOP community invoice attachment pages.

open_data_file_dataset_counts.name: SECOP II - Archivos Descarga Historico Hasta 2021

verification_model.received_publicly: A contract is marked received when SECOP II execution rows show received quantity with no public pending quantity or real progress near 100%.
verification_model.partial_public: A contract is marked partial when SECOP II execution rows show progress, received quantity, or real delivery dates, but not full completion.
verification_model.urgent_review: A contract is urgent when it has overdue/low execution progress, payment or invoices without visible delivery, repeated suspensions, or cancellation.
verification_model.financial_execution: Invoice, paid, and payment-plan values are reported as financial execution. They quantify billing/payment, not physical delivery.
verification_model.documentary_proxy: When there are delivery, supervision, or liquidation files but no SECOP execution rows, the delivery percentage is shown as a documentary-financial proxy using financial execution percent.
verification_model.pdf_text: Selected SECOP PDF attachments are downloaded with browser-like headers and processed with pdftotext. The MVP prioritizes contracts with fewer document rows, then the strongest delivery/supervision/payment files. When enabled, low-text PDF output is rendered with pdftoppm and read with Tesseract OCR. Readable text is stored as snippets; blank/low-text output is marked as OCR-unreadable or likely OCR-needed.
verification_model.evidence_documents: Files are classified from public SECOP file names into delivery/receipt, supervision/completion, liquidation/close, invoice/payment, planning/concertation, generic acta, or other support.
verification_model.community_invoice_attachments: For the highest-priority contracts, the builder opens SECOP community ContractDetailView and ContractInvoiceView pages and classifies supplier/buyer invoice attachments that do not appear in the Datos Abiertos file table.

limitations: SECOP execution rows are a public administrative signal, not field proof that every ration reached a child or household.
Absence of SECOP execution/document rows means the public feed does not show delivery evidence; it is not proof that delivery never happened.
The strongest verifier in SECOP II is the supervisor/interventor and the uploaded actas, informes, remisiones, invoices, and liquidations.
Some entities upload files with inconsistent names, so file evidence is intentionally conservative.
